reason: STEP C leans on GT-phi-contraction (||Phi(r)||<=||r||, i.e. ||Phi||=1 in the order-unit norm) as if it were a verbatim refs/ ground-truth leaf, but it is NOT. grep -rF of 'order-unit-norm contraction'/'order unit contraction' over refs/ returns ZERO hits, and the external's own Source field honestly flags it as a DERIVATION (not a quote): ||Phi||=1 is re-derived from GT-orderunit-def + monotonicity (GT-positive-monotone) + unitality (Phi(1)=1). Per the project doctrine 'a derivation = af node' (no asserted 'elementary'/'standard' step) and L1 'ground truth = byte-verbatim refs/ match', this load-bearing multi-premise derivation must be EITHER (a) an explicit child derivation node grounded in the in-scope leaves GT-positive-monotone + GT-orderunit-def + Phi-unitality (exactly as the SIBLING validated workspace lem-P-properties does at its node 1.1.1, which PROVES ||Phi||=1 rather than asserting it), OR (b) imported as part of a validated lemma's exported CONTRACT. Neither holds here: GT-phi-contraction is asserted as a bare external, and ||Phi||=1 is NOT in the GT-Pprops / lem-P-properties root contract (that contract lists only P^2=P, P(1)=1, real-linear, ||R-1||<=Ceta, delta<=Ceta, ||P||<=1+Ceta, delta-positivity). So the step is an ungrounded leaf. NOTE this is a constructive, easily-fixable gap, not a math error: the fact is true and the constant stays universal/dimension-free either way; indeed the use is even AVOIDABLE via the triangle inequality already available in-scope: ||Phi(r)|| = ||(Phi(r)-r)+r|| <= ||Phi(r)-r|| + ||r|| <= delta||r|| + ||r|| = (1+delta)||r|| (Step A + triangle), giving ||x+r|| <= (2+delta)||r|| and C=(2+delta), still universal & dimension-free. The rest of node 1.2 is verified sound: GT-jordan-schwarz (refs/vlw-2604.08380/paper.tex:557-560, VERBATIM, self-adjoint reading (Phi r)^2<=Phi(r^2) correct), the commutative-Jordan identity x o x - r o r=(x-r) o (x+r), GT-jb-submult (refs/hos/joa-m.md:2306-2310 VERBATIM), GT-orderunit-def perturbation sign (refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372 VERBATIM, ||z||<=c => z>=-c1 used correctly in both Steps C and D), GT-bh-cstar ||r^2||=||r||^2 (refs/hos/joa-m.md:458 VERBATIM), constant absorption (C delta+delta)=C delta universal, and the eta exponent (lower bound is O(eta), correct). FIX: add the in-scope derivation child for ||Phi(r)||<=||r|| (mirroring lem-P-properties 1.1.1) or switch to the triangle-inequality bound; then this node is airtight.
